hypotheses.name: Momentum + Volatility
hypotheses.description: Technical composite score weighted by inverse 20-day volatility. Higher weight to low-volatility technical signals — reward steady trends, penalize erratic price action.
hypotheses.rationale: Classic low-vol anomaly adapted to A-share: stable trending stocks outperform high-vol names on a risk-adjusted basis over T+3 horizon.

hypotheses.name: Derived Hidden-Signal Bundle
hypotheses.description: Hidden signal composite (H1-H9) as sole input. No price features, no volatility, no volume. Derives statistical features from the hidden-signal bundle — NOT non-price or alternative data.
hypotheses.rationale: If hidden signals carry genuine alpha uncorrelated with price, a pure hidden-signal model should show positive Rank IC independent of technical factors. Failure = hidden signals are just transformed price data.

hypotheses.name: Signal-Volume Interaction
hypotheses.description: Interaction term: signalCount × compositeScore. Tests whether the confluence of multiple signals (high signalCount) amplifies or dampens the composite score's predictive power.
hypotheses.rationale: When many signals agree (high signalCount), the composite score should be more reliable. The interaction term captures this amplification effect. Weak interaction = signal diversity doesn't improve reliability.
